# 🤝 Code of Conduct

_A respectful, practical, evidence-aware community standard for the EU AI Act Toolkit._

## Quick standard

| Expectation | Focus |
|---|---|
| ✅ Be respectful | constructive feedback and disagreement |
| ✅ Be practical | plain-language suggestions and evidence-aware updates |
| ✅ Be careful | cautious wording for legal and compliance claims |
| ⛔ No harassment | spam, aggressive conduct, or discrimination |
| ⛔ No data disclosure | confidential or personal data in issues/PRs |
| ⛔ No misleading claims | requests for legal advice on confidential facts |

<details>
<summary><strong>✅ Expected behaviour</strong></summary>

- constructive feedback
- plain-language suggestions
- source-aware updates
- respectful disagreement
- cautious wording for legal and compliance claims

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>⛔ Unacceptable behaviour</strong></summary>

- harassment
- spam
- confidential data disclosure
- personal data disclosure
- misleading compliance claims
- aggressive or discriminatory conduct
- using issues or PRs to request legal advice on confidential facts

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>⚖️ Legal and compliance wording</strong></summary>

Contributors should not claim that the toolkit guarantees compliance.

Use wording such as:
- readiness
- screening
- starting point
- documentation support
- escalation

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>📢 Reporting concerns</strong></summary>

- **Public content issues:** open a GitHub issue
- **Sensitive concerns:** use [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) for security, privacy, or confidentiality concerns
- **Data safety:** do not publish confidential or personal data in GitHub issues

</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>🔨 Enforcement and scope</strong></summary>

**Enforcement:** Maintainers may edit, close, label, or remove content that violates this standard.

**Scope:** This standard applies to issues, pull requests, discussions, and other project spaces.

</details>
